description_long: This object is a detailed model of a freshwater crayfish, featuring a robust exoskeleton with a segmented body divided into a cephalothorax and a long, flexible abdomen. The cephalothorax is covered by a hard carapace, from which two large, asymmetrical claws (chelae) extend, each with visible pincers and textured surfaces. The crayfish has multiple walking legs attached to the underside of the cephalothorax, and its coloration transitions from olive green and brown on the dorsal side to lighter, reddish hues on the ventral side. Two long antennae project forward from the head, and small, dark eyes are positioned on short stalks. The surface of the body appears hard and slightly rough, with subtle glossiness reflecting the natural chitinous shell.
description: A lifelike crayfish with a segmented, hard shell, large claws, and long antennae, colored in greenish-brown and reddish tones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crayfish, highlighting the large claws, segmented body, and walking legs.
description_view_1: Top-down view emphasizing the symmetry of the claws, the segmentation of the body, and the dorsal coloration.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the claws, legs, and body segmentation.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the claws, antennae, and the placement of the eyes on short stalks.
